summary: Solid soundboard capture with heavy drumming and Brent’s organ, highlighted by a fiery Samson & Delilah and trippy Wharf Rat despite a few audience‑only sections.
review: The recording is essentially a clean board mix, though the reviewer suggests skipping the audience‑only portions of “Promised Land” and “Loser.” Musically the show shines – a mellow Terrapin intro, a dancing Scarlet>Fire, a slide‑laden Samson & Delilah, and an expansive Wharf Rat jam that showcases each member’s improvisational spark.

ai_rating.rationale: The single detailed review praises the musical performance strongly while noting only minor recording hiccups, leading to a high show rating and a good‑but-not‑perfect recording score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall solid board sound with clear instrument separation; audience bleed on “Promised Land” and “Loser,” plus a brief dead‑air gap between first‑set songs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set builds a high‑energy groove with heavy drumming, Brent’s organ swells and the band splitting into exploratory directions before reconvening on Wharf Rat.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is noted weaving his guitar alongside Phil during the extended jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass lines drive the “Around” section and he’s described as heading one way in the improvisation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is mentioned as splitting the difference, contributing to the balanced rhythm section.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ work is highlighted, especially on the jam that leads into Wharf Rat.
band_member_comments.Drums: Heavy, funk‑laden drumming carries through the set and fuels the trippy space sections.
